steps.title: Find two complete sentences
steps.content: “For centuries, scholars dreamed about locating a copy of this legendary lost work” is a complete sentence. “That fantasy (partially) became reality in 2022, when researchers uncovered traces of the star catalog” is another complete sentence in the passage.

steps.title: Separate the sentences
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice. Choice B fuses the sentences with no punctuation. Choice D adds “and” without the needed comma and makes the sentence awkward. A period cleanly separates the dream from the later discovery. The answer is C.
